calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Continued To Oct-19-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Their Counsel Of Record In The Amount Of $3,300
Off Calendar Apr-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to compel deposition and for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART.

On March 13, 2026, Defendant served a fourth notice of deposition that set Plaintiff's deposition for April 3, 2026, April 7, 2026, and April 10, 2026. The parties agreed that the deposition can be conducted remotely. (Feder Decl., pars. 11-12, Ex. G.) Plaintiff states that these deposition dates and conditions meet their medical accommodation needs and they will appear.

Defendant has been attempting to depose plaintiff since June 6, 2025. (Young Decl., par. 2.) Plaintiff has not acted with substantial justification. The issues regarding the deposition dates and proposed accommodations should have been resolved months ago and the court concludes that Plaintiff and their counsel were principally responsible for the delay and need for this motion.

The court orders Plaintiff to appear for deposition on April 3, 2026, April 7, 2026, and April 10, 2026, consistent with Defendant's fourth notice of deposition.

The record does not demonstrate Plaintiff and counsel acted with substantial justification. Plaintiff L. Brown and counsel Law Offices of Daniel Feder shall pay $1,625 to Defendant as sanctions, payable no later than 20 days after notice of this order. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010, 2023.030, 2025.450(g).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
